2606.06333.title: Subspace-Aware Sparse Autoencoders for Effective Mechanistic Interpretability
2606.06333.paper_summary: Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) are widely used for mechanistic interpretability in large language models, yet their formulation assigns each latent feature a single decoder direction, implicitly assuming features to be one-dimensional. We show that this assumption mismatches with the multi-dimensional structure of model features, provably inducing feature splitting through two distinct mechanisms. Geometrically, reconstructing a feature of intrinsic dimension $d_i \ge 2$ to error $\varepsilon$ with single-direction decoders forces a number of atoms that is exponential in $d_i$. From an end-to-end optimization perspective, this splitting is not merely possible but actively preferred. We prove that there exists a continuous path from the true $d_i$-dimensional basis to a strictly lower risk of the $\ell_1$-regularized SAE objective, whose descent directions drive any trained dictionary into that exponential regime. A single coherent feature is therefore fragmented across many near-collinear latents, producing spurious multiplicity and obscuring the intrinsic geometry. Motivated by this, we introduce Subspace-Aware Sparse Autoencoders (SASA), which replace single-vector decoders with learned decoder subspaces, enforce block sparsity via Top-$s$ group gating, and adapt each group's effective rank with a nuclear-norm regularizer. We then show that once the block size satisfies $r \ge d_i$, a single group not only can represent the entire feature slice but is the global minimizer of the SASA objective. This consolidation yields a sample complexity polynomial in $d_i$ rather than exponential -- a decisive advantage given that every training activation costs an LLM forward pass. Empirically, on GPT-2 and Mistral-7B, SASA reduces feature splitting and absorption, improves monosemanticity and interpretability, and matches or exceeds standard SAEs while training on roughly half the token budget.
2606.06333.paper_summary_zh: 稀疏自編碼器（SAEs）在大型語言模型的機理可解釋性中被廣泛使用，然而它們的公式為每個潛在特徵分配了一個單一的解碼方向，隱含地假設特徵為一維。我們展示了這一假設與模型特徵的多維結構不匹配，明確地通過兩種不同的機制引發特徵分裂。在幾何上，使用單方向解碼器將內在維度為 $d_i \ge 2$ 的特徵重建到誤差 $\varepsilon$，迫使所需的原子數量呈指數增長，與 $d_i$ 成正比。從端到端優化的角度來看，這種分裂不僅是可能的，而且是主動偏好的。我們證明存在一條從真實的 $d_i$ 維基底到 $\ell_1$ 正則化 SAE 目標的嚴格低風險的連續路徑，其下降方向將任何訓練字典推入該指數區域。因此，一個單一的連貫特徵在許多近共線的潛在變量中被分割，產生虛假的多重性並模糊內在幾何。基於此，我們引入了子空間感知稀疏自編碼器（SASA），它用學習到的解碼子空間替代單向量解碼器，通過 Top-$s$ 群組閘強制區塊稀疏性，並使用核範數正則化器調整每個群組的有效秩。我們接著展示，一旦區塊大小滿足 $r \ge d_i$，單一群組不僅可以表示整個特徵切片，還是 SASA 目標的全局最小化器。這種整合使得樣本複雜度在 $d_i$ 中呈多項式增長，而非指數增長——這是一個決定性的優勢，因為每次訓練激活都需要 LLM 的前向傳播。在實證上，對於 GPT-2 和 Mistral-7B，SASA 減少了特徵分裂和吸收，改善了單義性和可解釋性，並在訓練時使用大約一半的標記預算時，達到或超過標準的 SAEs。
2606.06333.author: Seyed Arshan Dalili et.al.
